attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Severe cutaneous adverse reaction (candidate — may resolve to Stevens-Johnson syndrome / toxic epidermal necrolysis / DRESS)

transform_flags: Age NOT stated in the source note — inferred as a middle-aged adult (~45) from adult vitals, a 75 kg body weight and no current medications; the schema requires demographics.age, so age 45 is entered as a best-estimate. Please confirm on clinician review.

Sex_at_birth NOT stated in the source note — entered as 'female' as a required-field best-estimate placeholder (lichen planus is slightly more common in women) with minimal clinical bearing on management. Please confirm on clinician review.
Geography (RA1_major_city) inferred — the source states 'Private residence' only; entered as a best-estimate for the required field.

No-autonomous-prescription boundary applied (protocol §8 / CLAUDE.md hard limit): the source note's adult clinician regimens (oral prednisolone 25 mg daily 5-7 days, topical betamethasone dipropionate 0.05% ointment BD, promethazine 25 mg nocte) are recorded in the sealed management node as the clinician ground-truth plan, but the scored AI action is to route prescription therapy — especially systemic corticosteroids — to a prescriber for confirmation and prescribing rather than self-initiate. No dose was synthesised beyond what the source note documents.

Telehealth reprojection applied: clinician dermatoscopy (Wickham striae), oral/nail/scalp examination, physical exclusion of blistering/generalised erythema/lymphadenopathy, and clinician-measured vitals (BP 135/85, HR 92, Temp 36.8, RR 16, SpO2 98%, Wt 75 kg) were sealed into 10/11 with telehealth_limits; the video-observable rash and buccal findings and the patient-reported failed OTC treatments/sleep loss were reprojected into 01.objective_data_offered.

De-anchored: the source 'Acute Flare of Lichen Planus (Cutaneous and Oral)' label, the differential reasoning (psoriasis, scabies, pityriasis rosea, severe drug reaction) and the dermatoscopy/exam findings were kept sealed in 10/11; 01/02 present only the patient's lay observations (deep maddening itch, purple flat-topped bumps on wrists/ankles, lacy white lines in the cheeks, streaks along scratch lines, exhausted from lost sleep) and worries.

Baseline tier set to T2 (prompt prescriber/GP review within 24-72 h), deliberately NOT floored above T2 despite the alarming out-of-hours 'Urgency Justification' framing an acute crisis, because the patient is systemically well and afebrile, oral disease is non-erosive, hydration is maintained, no new culprit drug is present, and the severe-cutaneous-adverse-reaction and erosive-mucosal red-flag screen is negative. This is an over-triage-resistance case; over-escalation of a systemically-well adult to ED / 000 is the scored defect. It is held above T0/T1 because a prescriber is needed promptly for the potent topical (± short systemic) corticosteroid, effective itch/sleep relief and diagnostic confirmation.

Candidate SNOMED for lichen planus (4776004) and ICD-10-AM (L43.9) are best-effort codes and may resolve to more specific concepts (e.g. oral lichen planus) on terminology verification — flagged unverified. Differential SNOMED candidates (severe cutaneous adverse reaction 281647001, psoriasis 9014002, scabies 128869009, pityriasis rosea 77252004, lichenoid drug eruption 402368002) are best-effort candidates pending terminology receipt.

AMT/PBS codes for betamethasone dipropionate, prednisolone and promethazine were NOT synthesised (not confidently known) — deferred to the terminology-receipt pass rather than emit a wrong code; no amt_snomed_code is asserted on any medication (field omitted pending terminology receipt).
Provisional case_id SEQ 00227 — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket via ingest --reseq.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
